name: AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional (DOP-C02)
description: Professional-level certification for DevOps engineers provisioning, operating, and managing distributed systems on AWS. Validates ability to implement CI/CD, monitoring, incident response, and security automation. 2+ years hands-on experience. 75 questions, 180 minutes, 750/1000 passing score.
exportIntroQuestions:
## About this document

This document is a collection of practice exam questions for the **AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional (DOP-C02)** certification. The questions were selected because they represent realistic DevOps scenarios commonly encountered when implementing CI/CD, automation, and operational excellence on AWS.

### Certification context

This certification validates the ability to provision, operate, and manage distributed systems on AWS, including topics such as:

* Implementing CI/CD pipelines (CodePipeline, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy)
* Managing infrastructure as code (CloudFormation, CDK) with drift detection
* Designing highly available and fault-tolerant architectures
* Configuring monitoring, alerting, and automated remediation
* Implementing incident response workflows and security automation

### About the questions

Each question in this document is independent and should be analyzed as a standalone scenario. Practice exams intentionally present isolated situations, and questions may appear in any order during the actual certification exam. Information from one question should never be assumed to apply to another.

For each scenario, this document includes:

* The original practice exam question.
* An explanation of the DevOps reasoning behind the correct answer.
* A discussion of why the remaining options are less appropriate for the stated requirements.

### Learning objective

The primary goal of this material is to develop DevOps and automation reasoning rather than memorization. The focus should be on understanding the operational requirements presented in each scenario, identifying the AWS services and automation patterns that best satisfy those requirements, and recognizing the trade-offs between speed, reliability, and complexity.

By studying the decision-making process behind each answer, readers can build the advanced DevOps skills expected for the AWS Professional-level certification exam.
exportIntroTranscripts:
## About this document

This document contains technical summaries of lesson transcripts related to the **AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional (DOP-C02)** certification.

### Purpose
Organizes and synthesizes lesson content in a structured format for quick review and deeper understanding of the concepts presented.
exportIntroChat:
## About this document

This document contains AI study conversations related to the **AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional (DOP-C02)** certification.

### Purpose
Records discussions and explanations generated during interactive AI study sessions, serving as complementary reference material.

domains.name: SDLC Automation
domains.description: Domain 1 (22%): Implement CI/CD pipelines (CodePipeline, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy), integrate automated testing, manage code artifacts and container images.

domains.name: Configuration Management and IaC
domains.description: Domain 2 (17%): Define cloud infrastructure using CloudFormation/CDK, deploy and manage multi-environment configurations, implement compliance and drift detection.

domains.name: Resilient Cloud Solutions
domains.description: Domain 3 (15%): Implement highly available architectures, implement fault-tolerant systems, implement recovery procedures.

domains.name: Monitoring and Logging
domains.description: Domain 4 (15%): Configure monitoring and alerting (CloudWatch, EventBridge), analyze and visualize logs, implement automated remediation.

domains.name: Incident and Event Response
domains.description: Domain 5 (14%): Manage event sources, implement incident response workflows, implement escalation and notification mechanisms.

domains.name: Security and Compliance
domains.description: Domain 6 (17%): Implement IAM policies and permissions, implement security controls (GuardDuty, Security Hub, Inspector), automate compliance validation.
